Title: miR398-SlCSD1 module participates in the SA-H2O2 amplifying feedback loop in Solanum lycopersicum

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immune signaling, stress response, amplification of SA-ROS defense signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; repressed by low exogenous SA (0.01-0.5 mM); elevated in NahG plants with reduced SA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for pri-miR398b overexpression; endogenous MIR398b promoter used in LUC reporter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; dual-luciferase target validation with SlCSD1; qRT-PCR; SA quantification by HPLC-MS; H2O2 metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous salicylic acid treatment; endogenous SA reduction in NahG plants; normal growth conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Perturbation of sly-miR398b levels increases salicylic acid accumulation; overexpression lowers H2O2 whereas loss-of-function raises H2O2 and SlCSD1 expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sly-miR398b directly targets SlCSD1. 2. Low SA represses sly-miR398b expression. 3. Both overexpression and knockout of sly-miR398b increase SA accumulation, with knockout causing a stronger increase and activating the ICS route, while overexpression is associated with PAL-route gene induction.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for fine-tuning SA-ROS signaling and stress tolerance in tomato; manipulation requires caution because both increased and decreased miR398b can alter SA homeostasis and ROS balance
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces H2O2 signaling; knockout strongly elevates SA and H2O2, potentially disturbing redox homeostasis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a miRNA regulator, the extraction schema classifies only TF/signaling regulators under Regulation_Genes; this focal RNA is therefore placed in Common_Genes. The paper emphasizes a feedback role in SA-H2O2 amplification rather than direct nutrient quality improvement.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling intermediate that amplifies SA signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; induced by low SA; reduced in NahG plants; elevated in miR398b mutant
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for pri-miR398b in target validation context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; SOD activity assay; H2O2 and superoxide quantification; dual-luciferase assay for miR398b targeting
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.01 mM SA treatment; endogenous SA depletion in NahG background
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA-induced H2O2 accumulation and contributes to conversion of superoxide to H2O2; loss of function partially abolishes SA-induced H2O2 increase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCSD1 expression tracks SOD activity under SA treatment. 2. SlCSD1 is a direct target of sly-miR398b. 3. SlCSD1 knockout reduces basal and SA-induced H2O2 accumulation and increases superoxide.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate node to modulate ROS signaling intensity and SA-dependent stress responses in tomato
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing SlCSD1 lowers H2O2 signaling and may weaken SA-dependent defense amplification; altering ROS conversion may impact redox balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, the study centers on its ROS-signaling role rather than terminal nutritional metabolite biosynthesis; classified as Common_Genes per schema emphasis on transporter/storage/other non-pathway core genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the salicylic acid biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate committed precursor toward salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in SA biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling coupled with SA quantification in miR398b transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher SlICS1 expression is associated with strongly increased SA accumulation in miR398b knockout plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlICS1 expression is induced in Mut-miR398b plants. 2. The stronger SA increase in Mut-miR398b correlates with activation of the ICS pathway. 3. The paper proposes H2O2 generated downstream of SlCSD1 promotes SA synthesis via the ICS route.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be combined with miR398-SlCSD1 module tuning to increase SA-dependent stress resistance, but requires control of redox and defense-growth balance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced SA biosynthesis may impose defense-growth tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SlICS1 itself was monitored as a downstream pathway readout rather than directly manipulated in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate, initiating the PAL-derived SA pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool toward salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step for PAL route
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in sly-miR398b overexpression plants and also induced in Mut-miR398b plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling coupled with SA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased SA accumulation when miR398b levels are perturbed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPAL2 expression rises in miR398b-overexpression plants. 2. SlPAL2 is also induced in Mut-miR398b plants. 3. The study links altered SlPAL2 expression to PAL-pathway-associated SA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream marker or target for modulating SA production under stress
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher SA may compromise growth if constitutively activated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper explicitly ties PAL-pathway induction to SA accumulation under miR398b perturbation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ROS signaling and SA-ROS feedback amplification through post-transcriptional control module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses sly-miR398b transcription by binding TGACG motif in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed to function together with NPR proteins/NPR1 in SA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Mediator that links SA input to repression of miR398b, thereby releasing SlCSD1 expression and promoting H2O2 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; dual-luciferase transient assay; promoter motif mutation analysis; bioinformatic promoter analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: SA-induced according to Discussion/Supplemental Fig. S2E
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly inhibits miR398b promoter activity, enabling the miR398b-SlCSD1 module to promote SA-induced H2O2 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlTGA2 binds the TGACG motif in the sly-miR398b promoter. 2. SlTGA2, but not SlTGA1, strongly represses miR398b promoter activity in transient assays. 3. SlTGA2 is proposed as the SA-responsive transcriptional mediator of the miR398b-SlCSD1 feedback loop.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory target to reinforce SA-dependent defense and ROS signaling, though manipulation should avoid excessive defense activation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter broad SA-responsive defense transcription and ROS homeostasis, with possible growth-defense penalties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes TGA2 acts with NPR1/NPRs in the model, but direct physical interaction was not experimentally tested here.
